text: The surgeon identifies a section of the appendix and prepares to release a small part of it. The appendix is visible, and the surgeon follows its path, indicating a point where it can be cut. The upper portion is examined, but the limits of the appendix are not clearly visible, suggesting possible destruction.

The surgeon identifies the base of the appendix, which is slightly falling, and prepares to remove it. There is a visible adhesion that does not belong, which the surgeon points out and prepares to address.
